title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
description: In the first week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk took the stand in a crisp black suit and tie and argued that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had deceived him into bankrolling the company. Along the way, he warned that AI could destroy us all and sat through…

title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
description: Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore. This session from MIT Technology Review’s EmTech AI conference explores why security must be rethought with AI at its core, not layered on after…

title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
description: Companies are taking control of their own data to tailor AI for their needs. The challenge lies in balancing ownership with the safe, trusted flow of high‑quality data needed to power reliable insights. This conversation from MIT Technology Review’s EmTech AI conference examines how AI factories unlock new levels of scale, sustainability, and governance—positioning data…

title: The Download: a new Christian phone network, and debugging LLMs
description: Plus: Elon Musk has admitted that xAI trained Grok on OpenAI models.

title: Inexpensive seafloor-hopping submersibles could stoke deep-sea science—and mining
description: A company called Orpheus Ocean wants to go “deep for cheap.”

title: Trump’s mass firing just dealt another blow to American science
description: Ambitious research is on the chopping block following yet more cuts at the National Science Foundation.

title: A new US phone network for Christians aims to block porn and gender-related content
description: Launching next week on T-Mobile's network, the cell plan takes a nuclear approach to online safety.

title: Exclusive eBook: Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones
description: Access a subscriber-only eBook on a startling and fairly graphic pursuit of human longevity that poses concerns about the ethics of cloning.

title: This startup’s new mechanistic interpretability tool lets you debug LLMs
description: Goodfire wants to make training AI models more like good old-fashioned software engineering.

title: The Download: the North Pole’s future and humanoid data
description: Plus: Google, Microsoft, Amazon and Meta have all set AI spending records.

title: The Download: storing nuclear waste and orchestrating agents
description: Plus: Elon Musk says Sam Altman “stole a charity” at the OpenAI trial.

title: It’s time to make a plan for nuclear waste
description: With growing interest in nuclear power, handling waste should be part of the deal.

title: The Download: Musk and Altman's legal showdown, and AI’s profit problem | MIT Technology Review
description: Plus: OpenAI has ended its exclusive partnership with Microsoft.

title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future | MIT Technology Review
description: Elon Musk says he’s suing to save the company’s mission. The case could have huge consequences for OpenAI and the AI race.

title: The missing step between hype and profit | MIT Technology Review
description: Coding aside, even the best AI systems struggle to be economically viable in the workplace. What happens then?

title: Rebuilding the data stack for AI | MIT Technology Review
description: Enterprise AI hinges on high-accuracy outputs, requiring better data context, unified architectures, and rigorous measurement frameworks, says Bavesh Patel, senior vice president at Databricks, and Rajan Padmanabhan, unit technology officer at Infosys.

title: The Download: DeepSeek’s latest AI breakthrough, and the race to build world models | MIT Technology Review
description: Plus: China has blocked Meta’s $2 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus.

title: Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters | MIT Technology Review
description: The long-awaited V4 is more efficient and a win for Chinese chipmakers.

title: The Download: supercharged scams and studying AI healthcare | MIT Technology Review
description: Plus: DeepSeek has unveiled its long-awaited new AI model.

title: Health-care AI is here. We don’t know if it actually helps patients. | MIT Technology Review
description: The tools may be accurate, but that doesn’t necessarily mean they’ll improve health outcomes.

title: The Download: introducing the Nature issue | MIT Technology Review
description: Plus: Trump signaled he’s open to reversing the Anthropic ban.

title: Will fusion power get cheap? Don’t count on it. | MIT Technology Review
description: New research suggests that cost declines could be slow for the technology.
